reasoning: The forecast date is October 26, 2025, leaving only roughly one month of the Atlantic hurricane season. Historically, hurricanes with maximum sustained winds exceeding 160 mph are exceptionally rare this late in the season (the 1932 Cuba hurricane is the only historical example of an Atlantic storm reaching such intensity after late October, while recent late storms like 2020's Iota maxed out at 155 mph). Without any indication of a >160 mph storm occurring in September or October, the base rate of such an event in November is on the order of 1-2%. Since the question requires a probability between 0.05 and 0.95, I'm setting the forecast to the 0.05 lower bound.
